info.name: Supermarket API
info.description: Complete test collection for the Online Supermarket microservices project

item.name: Flow 1 — Guest Browsing (Public)
item.description: Verifies that product browsing and search work without login, and that placing an order requires authentication.

item.name: 1.1 GET products by category
item.description: Public endpoint — no login required. Returns products filtered by categoryId.

item.name: 1.2 GET all products
item.description: Public endpoint — returns the full product catalog.

item.name: 1.3 GET categories
item.description: Public endpoint — returns all product categories.

item.name: 1.4 SEARCH products (Elasticsearch)
item.description: Public search via Elasticsearch. Expected: 200 with matching products.

item.name: 1.5 POST order WITHOUT login → 401
item.description: Guest attempts to place an order. Expected: 401 Unauthorized from the gateway (request never reaches order-service).

item.name: Flow 2 — Customer Places an Order
item.description: Full order flow: login as regular user, place order, verify owner-only access, verify 403 for other users.

item.name: 2.1 LOGIN as user
item.description: Authenticates as a regular user. Sets ACCESS_TOKEN cookie. Expected: 200.

item.name: 2.2 POST create order
item.description: Authenticated user places an order. customerId is taken from JWT — NOT from the body. Expected: 201 with total price and order details.

item.name: 2.3 GET own order → 200
item.description: Owner reads their own order. Expected: 200 with order details.

item.name: 2.4 LOGOUT user
item.description: Clears the ACCESS_TOKEN cookie. Expected: 200.

item.name: 2.5 GET other user order → 403
item.description: After login as admin, tries to read user's order using admin token — this should succeed for admin. Test by logging in as a DIFFERENT regular user and trying to read orderId=1.

item.name: Flow 3 — Admin Manages Inventory
item.description: Full admin flow: login as admin, create/update/delete products, update order status, view all orders.

item.name: 3.1 LOGIN as admin
item.description: Authenticates as admin. Sets ACCESS_TOKEN cookie with ROLE_ADMIN. Expected: 200.

item.name: 3.2 POST create product (ADMIN)
item.description: Admin creates a new product. Expected: 201. Also triggers ProductCreatedEvent to Kafka → search-service indexes it.

item.name: 3.3 PUT update product (ADMIN)
item.description: Admin updates an existing product. Expected: 200 with updated data.

item.name: 3.4 PATCH order status → SHIPPED (ADMIN)
item.description: Admin updates order status. Expected: 200. Triggers OrderEvent to Kafka.

item.name: 3.5 GET all orders (ADMIN)
item.description: Admin views all orders in the system. Expected: 200 with full list.

item.name: 3.6 DELETE product (ADMIN)
item.description: Admin deletes a product. Expected: 204. Triggers ProductDeletedEvent to Kafka → search-service removes it from index.

item.name: Flow 4 — Defense in Depth
item.description: Proves both security layers work: gateway returns 401, service returns 403.

item.name: 4.1 DELETE product WITHOUT cookie → 401 (gateway)
item.description: No cookie sent. Expected: 401 from the GATEWAY — request never reaches product-service. Proves layer 1 works.

item.name: 4.2 LOGIN as regular user
item.description: Login as USER (not admin) to get a valid cookie.

item.name: 4.3 DELETE product WITH user cookie → 403 (service)
item.description: Valid cookie but ROLE_USER only. Gateway passes the request. product-service @PreAuthorize rejects it. Expected: 403. Proves layer 2 works.

item.name: Flow 5 — Kubernetes Self-Healing (Reference)
item.description: These are kubectl commands run in terminal during the presentation — not HTTP requests. Included here as documentation.

item.name: 5.1 [kubectl] Get pods
item.description: Run in terminal: kubectl get pods -n supermarket

item.name: 5.2 [kubectl] Delete order-service pod
item.description: Run in terminal: kubectl delete pod -l app=order-service -n supermarket — Kubernetes recreates it automatically.
